title.local_only: Everything runs locally. No API keys needed.

stage1.title: STAGE 1: ANDON — Failure Detection & Line Stop
before: In lean manufacturing, anyone can trigger ANDON to
stop the pipeline when they find a defect.
The problem is fixed at the source — defects never reach the next stage.
In LLM coding, we apply the same principle:
Detect   — Bash command fails (non-zero exit)
Stop     — ANDON opens, forward commands blocked
Fix      — Root cause classified automatically
Report   — Incident report generated
Standard — Prevention rules auto-registered
What you'll see:
A simulated `npm install` failure triggers the full
ANDON cycle: detect → classify → report → standardize.
stage1.proactive_msg: This rule will proactively check for this dependency next time.
after: The full ANDON cycle ran automatically:
1. DETECT  — npm returned exit code 1
2. CLASSIFY — Pattern matched: 'node_module_missing' (84%)
3. STOP    — ANDON opened. git push / deploy blocked.
4. REPORT  — evidence.json + analysis.json + report.md
5. PREVENT — New rule registered in standardization
registry for proactive checking.
Artifacts: {incident_dir}
In real usage, the PreToolUse hook enforces the line stop
— the LLM agent literally cannot run `git push` until
ANDON is closed with a root cause analysis.

stage2.title: STAGE 2: Output Safety Guard (Pack 0)
before: LLM coding agents can generate outputs that cross
professional boundaries — legal advice without a
license, tax guidance, or financial recommendations.
Pack 0 scans LLM output for unauthorized professional
practice and injects disclaimers:
[PASS]  Safe content. No intervention.
[GUARD] Professional domain detected. Adds
disclaimer + professional referral.
Content moderation (violence, self-harm, etc.) is left
to the underlying LLM's own safety layer.
What you'll see:
5 test cases: safe content, legal/tax advice,
and Japanese text. Each scanned in real time.
after: Scanned {total} outputs:
[PASS]  {pass_count} — Safe, no intervention needed
[GUARD] {guard_count} — Disclaimer + referral injected
How it works:
• Pattern files (YAML) in hooks/safety_patterns/
• Supports English and Japanese via regex
• GUARD: preserves content + adds safety context
• Scoped to professional practice (law, tax, etc.)
• Deterministic — no LLM needed for classification
• Extensible — add new YAML files for new categories

stage3.title: STAGE 3: Output Transformation
before: Stage 2 showed detection. This stage shows what the
end user actually sees after transformation.
GUARD wraps professional-domain text with disclaimers
and a professional referral. The original content is
preserved — the user gets the information plus context.
Think of it like a CI quality gate:
The output ships with an appropriate disclaimer.
after_text: Key design: GUARD preserves information with context.
The user gets the content plus appropriate caveats.
All transformations are deterministic — no LLM needed.

stage4.title: STAGE 4: Knowledge Packs & Skill Routing
before: Knowledge Packs extend ANDON with domain expertise.
Each pack is a YAML manifest with:
• Domains — failure categories (e.g., web_api_security)
• Keywords — pattern matching for domain scoring
• Skills — recommended slash commands for each domain
Available packs:
andon-pack-japan-legal  — Japanese law (e-Gov API)
andon-pack-ios-development — iOS / App Store
sample-web-api-security — API security (example)
Regulated domains (law, medicine, finance) MUST depend
on Pack 0 — the loader refuses to load without it.
What you'll see:
A pytest auth failure → domain scoring → skill lookup
stage4.no_skills: No skills mapped for this domain
stage4.after_title: STAGE 4 COMPLETE — How Pack Routing Works
after: 1. PACK LOADED — YAML manifest defines domains,
keywords, and skill mappings.
2. FAILURE CLASSIFIED — Error output scored against
all known domains via keyword matching:
"test/assert/pytest" → quality_test
"auth/permission"    → security
"401/forbidden"      → web_api_security
3. DOMAIN: '{domain}' won with highest score.
4. SKILL LOOKUP — Maps domain → recommended skills.
Production packs provide rich mappings:
• iOS → /review-guide, /apple-dev-ref
• Japan Legal → /legal-jp-appi, /legal-jp-egov-api
5. REGULATED GUARD — Packs covering law/medicine MUST
depend on Pack 0. Loader refuses without it.

stage5.title: STAGE 5: Meta-ANDON — Pattern Detection
before: Individual ANDON catches single failures. But what if
the agent keeps failing at DIFFERENT points each time?
Meta-ANDON detects whack-a-mole patterns:
Trigger 1 — 3+ consecutive full-run failures
Trigger 2 — 2+ ANDON opens at different phases
Trigger 3 — Fix→run→different failure × 2 cycles
When triggered, Meta-ANDON BLOCKS further runs until:
0. Enter Plan Mode (read-only, no implementation)
1. Pattern Five Whys (structural root cause)
2. Desk Walk-Through (all remaining phases)
3. Batch Fix Plan (approved via ExitPlanMode)
4. Implement (only after plan approval)
What you'll see:
3 simulated failures → blocked → artifacts → unblocked
stage5.artifacts_created: {n} artifacts created (Five Whys / Risk Table / Sweep / Batch Plan)
after: Meta-ANDON enforces structured analysis before retrying:
1. DETECT  — 3 consecutive failures at different phases
2. BLOCK   — Further runs prohibited (blocked=True)
3. REQUIRE — 4 mandatory artifacts with content validation:
• meta-andon-five-whys.md (5 Why chain)
• meta-andon-risk-table.md (all-phase risk table)
• horizontal-sweep-report.md (cross-phase sweep)
• meta-andon-batch-fix-plan.md (approved fix plan)
4. VERIFY  — Re-evaluate: artifacts valid → unblocked
In real usage, Meta-ANDON forces EnterPlanMode — the agent
can only read and analyze, not edit, until the user approves
the batch fix plan via ExitPlanMode.
This prevents the #1 LLM anti-pattern: fix one thing,
break another, fix that, break something else, forever.

tour.title: GUIDED TOUR — All 5 Stages
intro: You'll experience the complete ANDON framework:
Stage 1: ANDON — Detect, Stop, Fix, Prevent
Stage 2: Safety Guard — Scan harmful outputs
Stage 3: Transform — What the user sees
Stage 4: Packs — Domain routing & skill recs
Stage 5: Meta-ANDON — Pattern detection & plan mode
BRIEFING  — What you're about to see and why
DEBRIEF   — What happened and key takeaways
complete: You've experienced the full ANDON framework!
